title: The Problem — India's Duplicate Vote Challenge
description: In Indian elections, manual voter verification struggles to prevent impersonation and duplicate balloting at scale. The question was whether face recognition could replace that manual gate. A biometric check before the ballot is cast would enforce one-person-one-vote without human bottlenecks.

title: Surveying the Landscape — 14 Face Recognition Methods
description: A systematic review of 14 published studies benchmarked approaches across Haar Classifiers, LBPH, EigenFace, FisherFace, PCA, LDA, and deep learning (CNN, YOLOv3). The goal was to identify which hybrid would be most reliable for single-voter, real-time deployment — not just peak theoretical accuracy.

barChart.caption: Test accuracy across surveyed face recognition methods

title: Building the Pipeline — Haar Detection + LBPH Recognition
description: The system pipeline flows through four stages: (1) Pre-processing — grayscale conversion and noise reduction to boost algorithm accuracy; (2) Face Detection — Haar Cascade classifier using edge, line, and centre-surround Haar-like features, tuned via scaleFactor (image scale step) and minNeighbors (false-positive suppression); (3) Feature Extraction — Haar-like features drawn from a 24×24 sub-window (~162,000 possible features); (4) Face Recognition — LBPH histogramming the local texture to match against trained voter identities. All implemented in Python with OpenCV.

rows: Haar-like features require greyscale; reduces noise
Detects face region using scaleFactor & minNeighbors params
Histogram-based texture matching against voter database
Reduces face-space dimensions; best for controlled lighting
Separates individuals across classes; handles illumination variation

title: Results — What Single vs Multi-Face Testing Revealed
description: The hybrid Haar+LBPH model reliably distinguished facial from non-facial images and correctly authenticated individual voters. The critical finding was an edge case: multi-face images degraded recognition confidence, while single-face images produced stable results. For a Smart Voting System — where voters stand one at a time — this boundary condition validates the deployment model. LBPH's 93.33% result in the closest analogous domain (wearable visual-assist device) confirmed it outperformed FisherFace (86.67%) for real-world texture variation.

rows: Boundary condition: one voter at a time enforced
System doesn't false-positive on background objects

title: Presented at IEEE Smart GENCON 2021
description: The paper was presented at the 2021 International Conference on Smart Generation Computing, Communication and Networking (SMART GENCON), 29–30 October 2021, organised by IEEE Pune Section. The Haar Cascade + LBPH Smart Voting System was accepted as a contribution to the emerging field of biometric security infrastructure for democratic systems.
